english: Cs.: 'one who speaks too fast', Sch.: 'too loud'. — k'a-díg (kha dig) cork, bung, stopple. — k'a-nañ (kha nang) yesterday morning C. — k'á-nar-can (kha nar can) oblong. — k'a-niñ (kha ning) last year. — k'á-po (kha po) sometimes f. k'a (kha) 1. mouth, e.g. *k'á-po dúl-mo* (*kha po dul mo*) W., *k'á dúl-po* (*kha dul po*) C., tractable. 2. speech Mil. 3. bitter C. — k'á-lpágs (kha lpags) lip, gón-ma (gong ma) upper, óg-ma ('og ma) lower lip; W.: k'ál-pag (khal pag) (s) pág-c'e (pag che), dáb-c'e* (dab che) to smack. — k'á-spu (kha spu) hair of the beard, skra dañ k'á-spu (skra dang kha spu) hair of the head and beard, frq. — k'á-p'ó (kha pho) boasting, k'a-p'o-c'é (kha pho che) id. — k'a-pór (kha por) = p'ór-pa (phor pa), a cup. — k'a-p'yís (kha phyis) napkin. — k'á-ba (kha ba) v. below. — k'a-bád (kha bad) the humidity of the air or the moisture of the earth caused by snow. — k'a-búb (kha bub) mouth or face being turned downwards. — k'a-brág (kha brag) v. below. — k'a-rbád (kha rbad) Cs.: 'a boast, proud speech'; others: idle talk. — k'a-sbyáñ (kha sbyang) eloquence Mil., k'a-sbyáñ-po (kha sbyang po) eloquent, cf. k'a-y'áñ? (kha yang?) — k'a-ma-c'ár (kha ma char) sleet, rain and snow. — k'a-múr (kha mur) bit (bridle) Sch — k'a-rtsáñ (kha rtsang), k'a-sáñ (kha sang) 1. B. C. yesterday forenoon, k'a-rtsáñ-gi k'yís-pa (kha rtsang gi khyis pa) the boy that was here yesterday forenoon Mil. 2. W. (*k'ar-sáñ* (*khar sang*)) the day before yesterday; some days ago; *k'ar-sáñ za-nyí-ma* (*khar sang za nyi ma*) last sunday: *k'ar-sáñ (s)tón-k'a* (*khar sang ston kha*) last autumn. — k'a-ts'a (kha tsha) 1. bitter and acrid Med. 2. 'hot in the mouth' a. a very acrid sort of radish, e.g. horse-radish. b. aphthae, thrush, a disease of the mouth, incident to horses, cows, sheep. c. k'a-ts'á riñ-ñe-ba (kha tsha ring nge ba) Mil. nt. daily warm food. — k'a-ts'úb (kha tshub) snow-storm. — k'a-ts'ó (kha tsho) boasting, k'a-ts'ó dìn-tu c'é-ba (kha tsho din tu che ba) a great swaggerer Glr. — k'a-ts'ón (kha tshon) v. below. — k'a-mts'úl (kha mtshul) muzzle, mouth (of a dog etc.); the lower part of the human face col. — k'a-ts'óg (kha tshog) abuse? *k'a-ts'óg c'ém-po* (*kha tshog chem po*) C. a great abuser, reviler. — k'a-źán (kha zhan) the contrary of k'a-drág (kha drag), low, unimportant, having no authority, k'a-źán-pai sdug-bsñál (kha zhan pai sdug bsngal) the misfortune of being of low birth Mil. — k'a-źé (kha zhe) 1. 'mouth and mind', k'a-źé mi mts'úñs-pa (kha zhe mi mtshungs pa) hypocrisy, hypocrite C. 2. 'mouth-mind', meaning the same as the phrase just mentioned: hypocrisy Mil., k'a-źe-méd-pa (kha zhe med pa) unfeigned, sincere Mil. — k'a-źéñ (kha zheng) breadth, expanse, e.g. of the heavens Mil. — k'a-zás (kha zas) food, victuals B. C. — k'á-ya (kha ya) lit.: 'being one's partner or match as to speaking', also k'ai ya (khai ya), — gen.: partner; match; *k'á-ya jhè'-pa* (*kha ya byed pa*) C. to assist, *k'o k'e ya* (*kho khe ya*) (or *k'a-ya* (*kha ya*)) ñe mi t'ub* (*nge mi thub*) I am not his match, not able to compete with him; with regard to things: I am not equal to the task. — k'a-ras (kha ras) neck-cloth, cf. k'a-dkri (kha dkri). — k'á-ru-ts'a (kha ru tsha) alum Med. — k'a-rúd (kha rud) snow-slip, avalanche, — k'a-ró (kha ro) taste in the mouth. — k'a rog (kha rog) v. k'a (kha) IV. 1. extr. — k'a-lán (kha lan) 'mouth-requital' 1. thanks-giving Mil. 2. reply, esp. angry reply. — 3. requital for food received C. — k'a-leb (kha leb) cover, lid. — k'á-lo (kha lo) 1. 'mouth leaves', sñoi k'á-lo (sngoi kha lo) Mil. the young, tender leaves of several wild herbs, used as vegetables. 2. v. below. — k'a-śá (kha sha) 1. v. k'a-skyúr-po (kha skyur po). — k'á-śá śá-ba (kha sha sha ba) S.g., 'snow-deer', elk Sch.; shoe-leather from the skin of this animal is mentioned in Mil., and is known in Tibet. In Sik. however the deer of the neighbouring Tarai is called k'a-śa (kha sha), in other parts of the country the spotted deer, — k'a-śágs (kha shags) jest, joke, *k'a-śág t'áb-c'e, táñ-c'e* (*kha shag thab che, tang che*) W. to jest. — k'a-śúgs-can (kha shugs can), -śéd-can (-shed can) W. eloquent. — k'a-śés (kha shes) Cs. some. — k'a-śób (kha shob) col. lies, falsehoods; obscene talk; idle talk. k'a-bśád (kha bshad) talk, gossip Mil. — k'a-sáñ (kha sang) v. k'a-rtsáñ (kha rtsang). — k'a-siñ (kha sing) several weeks ago Cs. — k'a-só (kha so) mouth and teeth; similar: k'a-mgúl (kha mgul) mouth and jaw-bone, *k'á-só* (*kha so*) or *k'a-gál c'ag yin* (*kha gal chag yin*) I shall break your chops W. — k'á-sró? (kha sro?) Ld. *k'a-sró lám-c'e* (*kha sro lam che*) to fry (meat) in butter. — k'a-slób (kha slob), = k'a-tón (kha ton), learnt by heart, (used by children) W. — k'a-lhág (kha lhag) remnant of a meal Mil.
W. col. for k'a-c'e-yul (kha che yul), Cashmere.

Cashmere; amongst other things it produces much saffron, hence k'a-c'e-skyes (kha che skyes) saffron; in Cashmere Buddhism was once flourishing (v. the legend relative to its being introduced there: Introduction du Buddhisme dans le Kashmir
